articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

images.name: Lionel Messi doubles Argentina's lead from the penalty spot
images.caption: Lionel Messi doubles Argentina's lead from the penalty spot

images.name: Lionel Messi doubles Argentina's lead from the penalty spot

articles.headline: Martínez: Cristiano Ronaldo sets the example for Portugal
articles.description: Roberto Martínez says Cristiano Ronaldo is not thinking about his Portugal future beyond the World Cup.

images.caption: Roberto Martínez says Cristiano Ronaldo is not thinking about his Portugal future beyond the World Cup.

article.story:
Scintillating cameos from Nestory Irankunda, Jordy Bos and Riley McGree have delivered a 5-1 win over Curacao to send the Socceroos off to the World Cup on a powerful winning note.

After Awer Mabil opened the scoring in the 23rd minute, Australia's send-off looked set to fizzle into a night of disappointment when Curacao equalised in the 50th, while Ajdin Hrustic (hamstring) had limped off just before halftime.

But Tony Popovic turned to his big guns in Bos, McGree, Irankunda and Cameron Burgess in the 66th minute and it immediately paid off.

Two minutes later, Irankunda and McGree combined before the latter's wicked ball was emphatically nodded home by Alessandro Circati to restore Australia's lead.

McGree then worked the ball to Bos to dance through the area and unleash a superb right foot strike.

Bos then found Irankunda, who beat a defender then fired home at the near post in the 80th minute and backflipped in celebration.

Nishan Velupillay slipped through Irankunda to seal his double four minutes later in front of 16,764 fans at AAMI Park.

After the 1-0 win over Cameroon, Popovic swung five changes, starting Aziz Behich, Kye Rowles, Kai Trewin, Mabil and Hrustic - while sticking with 18-year-old central defender Lucas Herrington.

It marked Mabil's first start in two years while Trewin was tested in the right wing-back role for the first time.

Skipper and keeper Maty Ryan was called into action twice in the 11th minute, denying Tahith Chong and Brandley Kuwas.

For the opening goal, Hrustic brilliantly pressed Curacao skipper Leandro Bacuna into a loose pass, with Trewin cutting the ball off, bursting forward then squaring the ball for Juric.

The striker's flick-on took a deflection into the path of Mabil, who toe-poked home.

In first-half stoppage time, Mabil spurned a golden chance to snag a double when he met a terrific left-footed cross from Hrustic at the back post but volleyed into the woodwork.

Hrustic grabbed at his left hamstring after his cross and was immediately substituted.

Velupillay replaced Hrustic at halftime while Paul Okon-Engstler and Jason Geria replaced Connor Metcalfe and Herrington respectively.

But four minutes later, Arjany Martha was played onside by Geria as he latched onto a ball from Gervane Kastaneer, then burst away from Rowles and scored.

Aiden O'Neill and Mabil spurned good chances before Popovic turned to his bench - and the Socceroos turned the tide.

Once Circati headed Australia back in front, the Socceroos surged to a comprehensive victory.

Australia return to action against Mexico at the Rose Bowl in Los Angeles on May 30.
